# SimplyFuel Documentation Index

Welcome to the SimplyFuel (Kal) documentation! This index helps you find the right documentation for your needs.

## 📚 Documentation Structure

### 🚀 Getting Started

**[README.md](../README.md)**
- Project overview
- Quick start guide
- Tech stack summary
- Available scripts
- Basic project structure

*Start here if you're new to the project.*

---

### 🏗️ Architecture & Design

**[ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md)**
- System architecture diagrams
- Component hierarchy
- Data flow patterns
- State management deep dive
- Routing architecture
- Build & deploy process

*Read this to understand how the application works.*

---

### 💻 Development Guidelines

**[CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md)**
- Code style guide
- Component patterns
- State management practices
- Styling conventions
- Testing guidelines
- Git workflow

*Follow these guidelines when contributing code.*

**[CODING_STANDARDS.md](CODING_STANDARDS.md)**
- Detailed coding standards
- TypeScript best practices
- React patterns (good vs bad examples)
- Performance optimization
- Error handling patterns
- Anti-patterns to avoid

*Reference this for code quality standards.*

---

- Common code snippets

### 🗺️ Planning & Roadmap

**[ROADMAP.md](ROADMAP.md)**

### 🧠 AI Context (Repository Memory)

**Repository Memory: `/memories/repo/simplyfuel-base-instructions.md`**
- Comprehensive project instructions
- Tech stack details
- Key conventions
- Important notes for AI assistance

*This is automatically loaded when an AI assistant works on the project.*

## 🔍 Quick Navigation by Role

### New Contributors
1. Read [README.md](../README.md) for overview
2. Review [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for workflow
3. Reference [QUICK_REFERENCE.md](QUICK_REFERENCE.md) for examples

### Developers
1. Reference [CODING_STANDARDS.md](CODING_STANDARDS.md) daily
2. Use [QUICK_REFERENCE.md](QUICK_REFERENCE.md) for patterns
3. Check [ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md) for system understanding

### Project Managers
1. Review [ROADMAP.md](ROADMAP.md) for planning
2. Check [README.md](../README.md) for current features
3. Reference [ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md) for technical decisions

### Designers
1. Review [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) styling section
2. Check [QUICK_REFERENCE.md](QUICK_REFERENCE.md) for UI patterns
3. See [ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md) for design system

## 🛠️ Development Workflow

```
1. Setup
   └─ Follow README.md

2. Understand Architecture
   └─ Read ARCHITECTURE.md

3. Learn Conventions
   └─ Review CONTRIBUTING.md

4. Start Coding
   ├─ Reference QUICK_REFERENCE.md
   └─ Follow CODING_STANDARDS.md

5. Submit Changes
   └─ Follow Git workflow in CONTRIBUTING.md
```

---

## 📁 File Structure

```
SimplyFuel/
├── README.md                    ← Start here
├── .github/
│   ├── CONTRIBUTING.md          ← Development guide
│   ├── CODING_STANDARDS.md      ← Code quality
│   ├── QUICK_REFERENCE.md       ← Code snippets
│   ├── ROADMAP.md               ← Future plans
│   ├── ARCHITECTURE.md          ← System design
│   └── INDEX.md                 ← This file
├── src/
│   ├── routes/                  ← Page components
│   ├── components/              ← Reusable components
│   ├── lib/                     ← Business logic
│   └── hooks/                   ← Custom hooks
└── /memories/repo/              ← AI context
    └── simplyfuel-base-instructions.md
```

---

## 🔄 Documentation Updates

This documentation should be updated when:
- Architecture changes significantly
- New major features are added
- Coding standards evolve
- Development workflow changes
- New best practices are established

**Last reviewed**: July 6, 2026

---

## 🤝 Contributing to Documentation

Documentation improvements are welcome! When updating:

1. Keep it concise and actionable
2. Include code examples where helpful
3. Update the last reviewed date
4. Cross-reference related sections
5. Follow Markdown formatting conventions

---

## 💡 Tips

- **Bookmark this page** for quick reference
- **Search within docs** using Ctrl+F
- **Code examples** are meant to be copy-pasted
- **Update docs** when you learn something new
- **Ask questions** in PRs if docs are unclear

---

**Questions?** Open an issue or ask in discussions!
